content.title: TDSC in 2026: Japan’s Power-Device and Storage Repositioning
content.subtitle: Consolidation, SiC/GaN Innovation, and AI-Driven Demand
content.message: Toshiba Electronic Devices & Storage Corporation enters 2026 with a strategic focus on infrastructure electrification and AI-driven data storage growth.

content.title: Japan Semiconductor Market Re-Accelerates
content.subtitle: A larger domestic market creates stronger pull for TDSC
content.message: The Japanese market is projected to triple by 2034, reaching $175B, driven by national electrification goals.

content.title: Strategic Shock: Toshiba–ROHM–Mitsubishi Integration
content.subtitle: Structural realignment to build global scale
keyPoints: March 2026: MoU signed for discussions on integrating semiconductor/power-device businesses.
Parties: TDSC, ROHM, Mitsubishi Electric, and investment partners (JIP/TBJ).
Objective: Creating a technology foundation capable of competing in the global market.
Shift from fragmented domestic capability to an integrated national-scale platform.

content.title: Power Semiconductors: Si, SiC, and GaN Expansion
content.subtitle: Spanning EVs, Data Centers, and Infrastructure
keyPoints: Automotive: SiC die and modules in 750V and 1200V classes for EV drivetrains.
Data Centers: High-efficiency 3kW PSU reference designs using GaN/SiC FETs.
Industrial: Next-generation motor-control and UMOS 11 MOSFET families.
Infrastructure: High-voltage protection and isolation devices for grid-level systems.

content.title: Efficiency Proof: Next-Gen MOSFET & SiC Performance
content.subtitle: Measurable R&D performance improvements in 2026
content.message: Toshiba's U-MOS11-H and SiC Trench technologies show dramatic power-loss and resistance reductions.

content.title: Strategic Demand Pools: Automotive & Data Centers
content.subtitle: Alignment with Japan’s fastest-growing use cases
keyPoints: Automotive: Strong pull from EV drivetrain inverters, ADAS, and body electronics.
Data Centers: Critical demand for power efficiency in AI servers and thermal management.
Networking: 5G base stations and DC-DC converters account for 32.7% application share.
Industrial: Expansion in motor drives and high-precision automation controls.

content.title: Storage Trend: AI Workloads Keep HDDs Strategic
content.subtitle: Benefiting from AI-era capacity pressure
content.message: Global storage requirements are forecast to nearly double by 2029, reaching 20,000 exabytes.

content.title: Competitive Landscape: Major Japanese Players
content.subtitle: Toshiba's position in the domestic semiconductor ecosystem
keyPoints: Discrete/Logic/Analog: TDSC remains a global leader in power and storage.
Memory: Kioxia (NAND) remains a key related player.
Consolidation Group: Integration talks with ROHM and Mitsubishi Electric create a new power-giant.
Other Peers: Sony (Image sensors), Renesas (MCUs), Murata (Components).

content.title: Expert Quote: The New Era of Data Growth
content.subtitle: AI and cloud infrastructure drive storage opportunity

quote.text: For Toshiba and its partners, these trends present both significant opportunities and challenges as the industry adapts to the new era of data growth.
quote.speaker: Rainer W. Kaese
quote.title: Senior Manager, HDD Business Development

content.title: 2026 Strategic Takeaways for TDSC Japan
content.subtitle: Convergence of policy, scale, and infrastructure
body: Consolidation is the headline: Moving toward a national power-semiconductor champion.
Scale Advantage: Tapping into a Japan market growing at 15.8% CAGR.
Efficiency Lead: Technical proof shows 30-62% performance gains in SiC/MOSFET.
Storage Resurgence: AI infrastructure demand doubles the HDD/SSD capacity requirement.
Strategic Play: Focus on 'integrated competitiveness' rather than isolated device sales.
